title: Light Echo around SN 2014J in M82
main_points: Reports Hubble light echo images taken from 2014-11-06 to 2016-10-12; echo scattered off a dust cloud extending 300 to 1600 light-years from SN 2014J.

title: Hubble Shows Light Echo Expanding from Exploded Star
main_points: Confirms Hubble observed the expanding light echo over roughly 10 months to nearly two years after explosion; emphasizes interstellar dust reflection geometry.

title: Optical and NIR observations of the nearby type Ia supernova SN 2014J
main_points: Reports B-band maximum at JD 2456690.14; bolometric model fit indicates total ejected mass about 1.3 solar masses and Ni-56 mass about 0.6 solar masses.

title: Extensive HST ultraviolet spectra and multiwavelength observations of SN 2014J in M82 indicate reddening and circumstellar scattering by typical dust
main_points: Reports discovery on 2014-01-21.805 and notes best distance estimate to M82 of 3.3 Mpc; HST/WFC3 UVIS observed seven epochs between 2014-01-28 and 2014-03-07.
